title: Begonia trichopoda
uses: Begonia trichopoda has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and inflammation. The plant's leaves are said to possess antimicrobial properties, making them useful against certain types of bacteria and fungi. In some regions, the leaves are used to make a tea that helps to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Additionally, the plant's stems and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and improve circulation. However, further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal properties and their potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Begonia trichopoda is a member of the family Begoniaceae. This genus is part of the order Cucurbitales. The family Begoniaceae is within the clade superrosids. The order Cucurbitales belongs to the clade rosid I. The clade rosid I is part of the clade core eudicots.
